title: Abelmoschus pungens
taxonomy: Abelmoschus pungens is classified in the kingdom Plantae, specifically in the family Malvaceae and the genus Abelmoschus. It belongs to the order Malvales and the clade Rosidae. At the rank of family, Abelmoschus pungens is most closely related to species such as Hibiscus sabdariffa and Gossypium hirsutum. Abelmoschus pungens is further classified as a species within the genus Abelmoschus, which comprises several other species native to tropical regions. The classification of Abelmoschus pungens is also reflected in its shared characteristics with other members of the Malvaceae family.
uses: Abelmoschus pungens is a plant species with potential medicinal uses, although more research is required to confirm its efficacy. It is reported to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may contribute to its therapeutic potential. Traditionally, the plant is used to treat various health conditions, including fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems. Its leaves and roots are often used in folk medicine to prepare remedies for these conditions. Further studies are needed to fully understand the medicinal properties and potential applications of Abelmoschus pungens.

preparations: Abelmoschus pungens, also known as the bitter gourd or spiny okra, has been used in traditional medicine for centuries.
